data.name: The Morning Minimalist
data.tagline: Quick, consistent, and out the door
data.description: You appreciate good coffee but your morning routine is a well-oiled machine. You want something that delivers a great cup quickly and consistently without requiring you to become a barista. Ease of use and reliability are your top priorities. You might enjoy a simple espresso or Americano before heading to work.
painPoints: Do not want to spend more than 5 minutes on morning coffee

data.name: The Craft Home Barista
data.description: For you, making coffee is a craft and a hobby, not just a caffeine delivery mechanism. You watch YouTube channels about extraction theory, you have opinions about water chemistry, and you have a spreadsheet tracking your dial-in process. You want equipment that rewards skill and lets you explore the full depth of espresso science.
priorities: Ability to grow into the equipment
painPoints: Current equipment is limiting further progress
Wants to replicate cafe-quality results at home

data.name: The Office Manager
data.description: You are responsible for keeping the office running smoothly, and good coffee is a morale booster your team cares about. You need something that can handle high volume, requires minimal training for everyone to use, and does not need constant maintenance. Reliability and throughput are your key concerns.
painPoints: Previous office machines broke down frequently
Needs something that multiple untrained people can use

data.name: The Traveling Professional
data.description: You travel frequently for work and have suffered through one too many terrible hotel room coffee makers. You want a portable solution that fits in your carry-on and lets you enjoy real espresso wherever you are. Weight, size, and independence from electricity are key factors in your decision.
painPoints: Hotel and airport coffee is unacceptable
Limited access to quality coffee while traveling
Does not want to depend on finding a good cafe

data.name: The Upgrade Seeker
data.description: You have been making espresso at home for a while on entry-level equipment and you are hitting a ceiling. Your current machine or grinder is holding you back and you can taste the difference at your favorite cafe. You are ready to invest in a meaningful upgrade that will unlock the next tier of quality.
priorities: Significant quality improvement over current setup
Equipment that will last many years
Features to grow into (flow control, pressure profiling)
Better temperature stability and grind consistency

data.name: The Gift Buyer
data.tagline: The perfect gift for a coffee lover
data.description: You are shopping for someone who loves coffee. Maybe it is a holiday, birthday, housewarming, or wedding gift. You want something that will genuinely delight them, looks beautiful, and matches their experience level. You might not be a coffee expert yourself, so clear guidance on what to buy is essential.
priorities: Appropriate for the recipient's skill level
Great value for the price point
painPoints: Not sure what the recipient already has
Wants to give something meaningful, not generic

data.name: The Latte Art Enthusiast
data.description: You are drawn to the visual artistry of latte art. You have watched countless tutorials and you are chasing that perfect rosetta, tulip, or swan. For you, the steam wand is just as important as the group head, and you need a machine that produces consistent, silky microfoam every time.
priorities: Dual boiler for simultaneous brew and steam
Quality milk pitcher with latte art spout
painPoints: Current machine steam wand lacks power or control
Single boiler machines cool down too much between brewing and steaming
Milk scorches before reaching proper texture

data.name: The Budget-Conscious Beginner
data.tagline: Great coffee without breaking the bank
data.description: You want to start making better coffee at home and you have heard that home espresso can save money compared to daily cafe visits. You have a limited budget but you do not want to buy something you will outgrow in six months. You are looking for the best value entry point that still delivers genuinely good coffee.
priorities: Best quality within a tight budget
Not something you will outgrow too quickly
painPoints: Worried about buying the wrong thing
